relationship.description: A restaurant owns many tables; each table belongs to one restaurant
A customer can make multiple reservations
Each reservation has one status; many reservations can share the same status (e.g., Confirmed, Cancelled)
A reservation can be linked to one or more reservation‑table rows (i.e., one or more tables)
A table can appear in many reservation‑table rows over time

metadata.improvements_made: Removed non‑essential attributes (is_outdoor, loyalty_points) for a leaner schema. Added explicit foreign‑key attributes (restaurant_id in TABLE, customer_id and status_id in RESERVATION). Clarified primary‑key composition for RESERVATION_TABLE (reservation_id, table_id) and noted it as the associative entity for the many‑to‑many relationship between reservations and tables. Corrected the cardinality between RESERVATION and RESERVATION_STATUS to N:1. Added a UNIQUE constraint concept for (restaurant_id, table_number) to enforce real‑world uniqueness (not shown in JSON). Retained all strengths of the previous comprehensive design.
